2026-04-06T09:35:23.0543976Z  ## Run tests in record mode
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0544183Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0545553Z -Before running tests, it's advised to update the dependencies and build our project by running the command `pnpm install && pnpm build --filter=<package-name>...`. Please notice this command is time-consuming and it will take around 10 mins, you could refer [here](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#resolving-dependency-version-conflicts) for more details.
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0548298Z +Before running tests, it's advised to update the dependencies and build our project by running the command `pnpm install && pnpm turbo build --filter=<package-name>...`. Please notice this command is time-consuming and it will take around 10 mins, you could refer [here](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#resolving-dependency-version-conflicts) for more details.
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0549752Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0549904Z  ```Shell
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0700812Z  > pnpm install
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0701193Z -> pnpm build --filter=@azure-rest/purview-datamap...
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0701687Z +> pnpm turbo build --filter=@azure-rest/purview-datamap...
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0702073Z  ```
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0702287Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0702922Z  Then, we could go to the project folder to run the tests. By default, if you don't specify `TEST_MODE`, it will run previously recorded tests.

2026-04-06T09:35:23.0866026Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0866201Z  ### InteractiveBrowserCredential
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0866433Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0866683Z -The `InteractiveBrowserCredential` follows the [implicit grant
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0867227Z -flow](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/v2-oauth2-implicit-grant-flow)
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0867817Z -which enables authentication for clients that run completely in the browser. It
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0868318Z -is primarily useful for single-page web applications (SPAs) which need to
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0868754Z -authenticate to access Azure resources and APIs directly.
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0869163Z +The `InteractiveBrowserCredential` follows the [authorization code
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0869684Z +flow](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/v2-oauth2-auth-code-flow)
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0957315Z +which enables authentication for clients that run in the browser or on Node.js.
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0957880Z +On Node.js, it opens a browser window while listening for a redirect response
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0958391Z +from the authentication service. In browsers, it authenticates via popups or
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0958855Z +redirect depending on the configured `loginStyle`.
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0959149Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0959429Z  To use this credential successfully, your app registration will need to be
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0966473Z -configured with both the **Access tokens** and **ID tokens** options checked under
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0967175Z -**Implicit grant** in the **Authentication** page.
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0967713Z -
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0968248Z -You will also need to add a redirect URI in the **Redirect URIs** section of the
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0968972Z +configured with appropriate redirect URIs. For Node.js, add a redirect URI
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0969493Z +for your desktop application in the **Mobile and desktop applications** section.
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0970198Z +For browser-based use, add a redirect URI in the **Redirect URIs** section of the
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0970718Z  **Authentication** page for your app registration. The redirect URI must point
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0971420Z  to the URI of your web application. You must also make sure to specify the same
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0971914Z  URI in the `redirectUri` field of the `InteractiveBrowserCredentialOptions` when
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0972336Z  creating an `InteractiveBrowserCredential`.
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0972930Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0973403Z -> NOTE: At this time, this credential can only be used in the browser but
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0974037Z -> Node.js support will be added in the future (see issue [#4774](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/4774)).
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0974535Z -
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0974703Z  ### DeviceCodeCredential
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0974902Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0975156Z  The `DeviceCodeCredential` follows the [device code authorization
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0975656Z @@ -314,7 +311,12 @@ which tries each of the following credential types in order until one of them
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0976059Z  succeeds:
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0976216Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0976378Z  - `EnvironmentCredential`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0976612Z +- `WorkloadIdentityCredential`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0976854Z  - `ManagedIdentityCredential`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0977089Z +- `VisualStudioCodeCredential`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0977322Z +- `AzureCliCredential`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0977536Z +- `AzurePowerShellCredential`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0977784Z +- `AzureDeveloperCliCredential`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0978169Z  
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0978456Z  This credential type is ideal when one of the credentials in the chain will work
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0978954Z  in the current environment, whether it's your local development or a production
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0979558Z diff --git a/documentation/writing-performance-tests.md b/documentation/writing-performance-tests.md
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0980234Z index b64522958..8a1fdabdd 100644
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0980520Z --- a/documentation/writing-performance-tests.md
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0980839Z +++ b/documentation/writing-performance-tests.md
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0981289Z @@ -252,7 +252,7 @@ Example: Currently `@azure/<service-sdk>` is at 12.4.0 on master and you want to
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0981959Z  - In the track 2 perf tests project, update dependency `@azure/<service-sdk>` version in `package.json` to `12.2.0`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0982497Z  - `pnpm install` (generates a new pnpm-lock file)
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0982825Z  - Navigate to `sdk\storage\perf-tests\<service-sdk>`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0983228Z -- `pnpm build --filter @azure-tests/perf-<service-sdk>...`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0983756Z +- `pnpm turbo build --filter @azure-tests/perf-<service-sdk>...`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0984448Z  - Run the tests as suggested before, example `npm run perf-test:node -- TestClassName --warmup 2 --duration 7 --iterations 2 --parallel 50`
2026-04-06T09:35:23.0985012Z
